Based on the provided evidence, let me reiterate my stance:
Reviewing the gathered information again confirms that the calcification of third molars generally begins much earlier compared to what was initially thought. Specifically, the anatomy text mentions that third molars begin to develop around the age of eight, though they do not always emerge until well into adulthood.
